title: gymnadenia austriaca subsp. iberica
intro: Gymnadenia austriaca subsp. iberica, also known as the iberian gymnadenia, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the orchid family (Orchidaceae), typically found in the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities, which are utilized to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. The iberian gymnadenia has been found to have various health benefits, including its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
